dialogos.texto_en: Hey, I'm here.
Futaro.
Welcome, Fu-kun.
Please come in!\nIt's been a while since you came to visit, Uesugi-san.
Visit, you say.
I've never come here just to visit.\nI came to do my job as a private tutor.
Even so, it's been a while, right?
...You've already graduated\nand finished your entrance exam preparations.\nThere's no need for me to come.
Well, thanks to you, we managed to get through it all.
But wait, you still don't know yet, do you.
No, but it's basically decided, right? \nYou took care of us, after all.
If you pass the special exam that's coming up,\nyou'll graduate, right?
Yeah. I was treated as being on a leave of absence,\nbut it was a special measure.\nThe school was considerate in many ways.
But if you fail, you'll be held back without mercy.\nBe aware that the situation\ndoesn't allow for any complacency.
Okaaay.
You're coming all the way to the office\nto work as a private tutor,\nso we can't waste any time.
Why don't we just do it at our place\ninstead of the office?
...It'd be hard to concentrate on studying\nwith just Ichika here.
Is that so?
Uesugi-san, as long as you're here, it'll be fine!\nAfter all, I was able to get into university\nthanks to you.
I also got into the school I wanted to\nthanks to Uesugi-kun.\nI'm sure you'll be fine too, Ichika.
And Uesugi-san himself has passed with flying colors.
That's right. Fu-kun is going to a university in Tokyo,\nso he won't be here anymore.
Nino and I are going to a culinary school,\nso it's finally time for our new lives to begin.
We'll each be moving in different directions\nand building new relationships in new places.
(A new life and new relationships are about to begin.\n But our relationship hasn't progressed at all.)
(As I devoted myself to studying for the entrance exams\n at the beginning of the year,\n emotions began to swell up inside me.)
(Can I really keep going like this?)
I really love you five.
I hope we can stay in this same relationship forever,\nthe six of us.
(But can we really \n 'stay in this same relationship forever'?)
Uesugi-kun, I'll speak my mind here without hesitation.
Not giving us an answer is the same as running away.\nThat's the only way I can feel about it.
(Wasn't it just as Itsuki said?)
(If I'm the only one going to Tokyo\n while leaving their respective feelings hanging\n like this, it's like I'm running away, isn't it?)
(I had decided not to choose anyone, but%p-2;――%p;)
(As a man, I've just got to come up with an answer.)
(That's why I came here today.\n But, how should I bring it up...?)
Uesugi-kun.
Y-yeah, what is it?
So, what's the purpose of your visit today?\nIt seemed like there was something important\nyou wanted to talk about.
You chose a time when I could get away from work,\nas you wanted me to be here as well, right?
Yeah.\nSorry, it's just that I'm busy with studying.\nI really wanted to talk when everyone was together.
Hmmm.\nIt seems like a pretty important topic.\nLet's hear it.
Well, it's about spring break.\nI want the six of us to go on a graduation trip together.
Huh?
(And, I want to make this graduation trip a stage\n to give them an answer%p-2;――%p;)
(Of course, if I'm refused,\n I have to think of another way.)
We don't know if Ichika will graduate yet,\nbut if it works for you guys, how about it?
What's with that reaction?\nIs it such a shocking proposal?
Ah, look, I know I'm not the kind of person\nwho says these kinds of things!
Oh, no, it's not like that! I was just surprised!
We were also thinking the same thing.
Huh? Really?
I was planning on proposing the idea\nafter I heard what you wanted to talk about today.
What do you mean by that...?
Well, there's no deep meaning to it.\nIf anything, it's about making memories, you know?
Making time for the six of us in the future\nis going to be difficult,\nso let's make lots of fun memories here!
I see...
What about you?\nWhat were you thinking\nwhen you suggested a graduation trip?
N-nothing in particular!\nI was also thinking about making one last memory!
...Is that so?
Well, it's decided then!\nWow, that was quick. What a relief.
We were thinking about going to Okinawa,\nis that okay?
Okinawa?\nI think it's a good place, but...
Is there a problem?
The travel fees. To be honest, they'll be tough.
Hmm, I see.\nWell, I guess we can split the bill.
Our father will arrange the accommodations, though.
Huh?\nSo you've already gotten your father's permission?
Yep, that's right.
...You seem to be oddly prepared.\nWhat were you going to do if I refused?
We would have dealt with it.\nYou don't need to worry about the details.
We'll decide on all the details. On the day\nof the trip, just bring some clothes\nand other necessities. Okay, Uesugi-san?
Got it... Thanks.
Leave it to us!
(The graduation trip has been decided,\n but what is this strange discomfort?)
(No, I shouldn't be thinking\n about unnecessary things right now.)
(The stage is set. However,\n the words that I should declare to them\n on said stage remain unclear.)
(Before this graduation trip...\n I have to find the answer to this difficult question%p-2;――%p;)
